{{Quote|Skinker! It's payday!|Beilert Valance|<ref name="marvel27" />}}
'''Skinker''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Skinker's salvage yard|salvage yard]] owner on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Feriae Junction/Legends|Feriae Junction]], who became acquainted with notorious [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. A committed hater of [[Droid/Legends|droids]], Valance paid Skinker handsomely to acquire scavenged automatons for him to destroy. When [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]—the object of Valance's obsessive hunting—passed through his yard, Skinker immediately informed Valance, only to get caught in the ensuing firefight and killed.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Polyp! Go get ''Valance!'' Tell 'im ol' Skinker has come across a likely lookin' ''prospect—very'' likely!|Skinker to Polyp, upon the arrival of Luke Skywalker|<ref name="marvel27" />}}
[[File:ValanceSkinker.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Beilert Valance and Skinker.]]
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Skinker owned a [[Skinker's salvage yard|salvage yard]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Feriae Junction/Legends|Feriae Junction]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Considered old by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Skinker formed an arrangement with [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]], an ex-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who spent several [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] plying his trade on Feriae Junction. Valance—driven by an virulent hatred of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and obsessed by tracking down an unnamed "[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|farmboy]]" who had played a crucial part in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] victory at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]—on several occasions paid Skinker to purchase droids from scavenger [[Starship/Legends|ships]] for him to destroy, specifically asking for [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO]] or [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series]] units such as those owned by his target. Although Skinker had no particular ire towards droids, Valance paid the going rate for rebuilt droids, so he continued to entertain the unhinged bounty hunter.<ref name="marvel27" />

In the meantime, Skinker also kept an [[Eye/Legends|eye]] out for the Rebel that Valance was so set on capturing, as the hunter had a hunch that his target may pass through Feriae Junction due to the [[Yavin blockade|Imperial blockade]] on the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]]. Sure enough, the young Rebel—[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]—turned up in Skinker's salvage yard, looking for parts to repair his damaged [[R2-D2/Legends|R2 unit]]. Skinker immediately recognized the man, who was accompanied by his [[C-3PO/Legends|3PO]] [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], and directed one of his employees, [[Polyp]], to pass the word along to Valance. Polyp delivered the message and returned to Skinker, but their incessant whispering drew the suspicion of Skywalker, who drew a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] on the pair and ruined Valance's efforts to get the drop on him. Skywalker managed to close Valance behind a a [[Magnetic seal/Legends|magnetically sealed]] door, only for the bounty hunter—who kept his [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] parts a closely guarded secret—to blast through it with his arm-mounted [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]], which killed Skinker and Polyp.<ref name="marvel27" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Well, I wouldn't share my ''spice'' with one... or want it next to me at the cantina. But they're useful... just like tools or blasters. Long as they keep their place... I can't see hating 'em. Not like you do, Valance.|Skinker, regarding droids|<ref name="marvel27" />}}
A man who made the repair and sale of salvaged droids one of the focuses of his business, Skinker held no particular dislike towards mechanicals, seeing them as useful parts of life in the modern world. However, while he considered Valance's destruction of the droids he acquired a waste of good circuitry, Skinker had little objection towards their arrangement as long as the hunter kept paying him handsomely. Skinker was loyal enough to his acquaintance to hurriedly contact him when Luke Skywalker turned up in his salvage yard, an action that led to his death.<ref name="marvel27" />

==Behind the scenes==
Skinker first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 27|''Star Wars'' (1977) 27]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1979]], where he was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]].<ref name="marvel27" />
